codeReview.summary: The feature satisfies its contract for frontend-write-path: the commit installs `wagmi`, `@rainbow-me/rainbowkit`, and `@tanstack/react-query`; adds a root `Providers` wrapper with `WagmiProvider`, `QueryClientProvider`, and `RainbowKitProvider`; wires a `ConnectButton` into the app header; and defines a custom Polkadot Hub TestNet chain from the canonical manifest in `src/lib/wagmiConfig.ts`. `src/lib/manifest.ts` already points at the canonical deployment, so the wallet stack is anchored to the correct chain metadata and unblocks later write-path work. I did not find any blocking issue for VAL-UI-001/VAL-UI-002 or the follow-on write forms, but there is one non-blocking gap in the exposed wallet options.

issues.description: `WALLETCONNECT_PROJECT_ID` is hardcoded to the placeholder string `DUALVM_LENDING_HACKATHON`. Because `getDefaultConfig()` advertises WalletConnect-backed options in the RainbowKit modal, those paths will fail until a real Reown/WalletConnect project ID is supplied, and they can emit avoidable console/network noise. This does not block the milestone because injected/MetaMask connections still have the correct custom chain configuration, but the modal currently exposes partially non-functional wallet choices.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared environment notes do not record that the committed wallet setup intentionally uses a placeholder WalletConnect/Reown project ID, so future frontend workers or reviewers may waste time rediscovering why WalletConnect-style options fail or why related console noise appears.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/handoffs/2026-03-17T02-31-35-018Z__frontend-wallet-connection__2e40150e-d01b-4be8-98c0-1e14f13854e0.json:52-55` explicitly notes that `WALLETCONNECT_PROJECT_ID` is the placeholder `DUALVM_LENDING_HACKATHON` and that WalletConnect modal connections will fail without a real ID, but `/home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/library/environment.md:23-34` contains no frontend wallet-provider note.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill should clarify that this repo's existing `npm test` Hardhat suite is still part of frontend verification because it covers shared frontend modules (manifest/read-model/barrel exports). The worker followed the procedure, but the handoff still had to explain that nuance manually.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md:45-48` says `npm test` only 'if tests exist for frontend modules', while the worker's handoff at `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/handoffs/2026-03-17T02-31-35-018Z__frontend-wallet-connection__2e40150e-d01b-4be8-98c0-1e14f13854e0.json:58-62` explicitly suggests clarifying that the existing Hardhat suite covers frontend module exports.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, skill guidance, and commit diff. The wallet provider stack and custom Polkadot Hub TestNet chain are wired correctly for the frontend-write-path milestone; the only issue I found is the non-blocking placeholder WalletConnect project ID behind some modal options.
